author: Besting
text: AFFECTED (light) by config/store split: the §7 binding shape is now { remote, tasks_branch, store, landing, invocation_path } (was {remote, branch, operating, invocation_path}); the reliable invocation-getcwd field this task adds composes with that binding. bl-2e26 §7; see bl-f970/bl-8c0b for the binding rename.

author: Bubbly
text: ARCH UPDATE (2026-06-06 config/store split + spec freeze docs/architecture.md): (1) Engine wiring is DONE — bl-35f7 wired run()→mutate::run for all mutating verbs; the delivery binding is produced end-to-end on close/drop/unclaim. Old blocker bl-bdeb re-closed (superseded). (2) Binding is being RESHAPED: §7 binding is now {remote, tasks_branch, store, landing, invocation_path} (was {remote, branch, operating, invocation_path}) — owned by bl-f970 (substrate) / bl-8c0b (delivery binding consumers); this ball now deps bl-f970 so the getcwd field lands on the FINAL shape. invocation_path SURVIVES and is still the normalized project-root that derives the worktree, so the getcwd-distinct-from-invocation_path rationale still holds. (3) FREEZE TENSION: §11 calls the cwd-guard 'best-effort... not a core guarantee' and §15 lists NO open topic for a getcwd field — so this ball is post-freeze scope-creep; its fate (cut / reframe as a bl-b465 open topic / implement) is undecided. (4) DOC DIVERGENCE: §11 describes the guard on close.post only; bl-4d00 generalized it to every teardown hook (close.post + unclaim/drop.post) — §11 needs that coherence fix.
